# GenSpark AI master prompt

Paste the block below into GenSpark Slides AI (or Gamma, Tome, or any one-shot slide generator). It bakes in the brand, the voice, and the 28-slide deck. Output is a complete deck in one generation.

---

```
You are designing a 28-slide keynote deck for Daniel Paul, founder of Purely Personal. The deck is titled "How to build a brand that brings high-ticket buyers to you." It is being delivered today as a paid 60-minute teaching session to a room of business owners. Daniel is not pitching anything — the session is pure value.

DESIGN SYSTEM (non-negotiable):

Canvas: 1920 × 1080 (16:9).
Colors: only these five.
  Near-black background: #0a0a0a
  Off-white background: #f7f7f8
  Red accent: #e90d41 (use sparingly, max 5% of any frame, only on highlights, eyebrows, key words, dividers)
  Silver secondary text: #b8bec1
  White card fill: #ffffff
Typography:
  Headings: Rethink Sans, weight 700, letter-spacing -0.02em, line-height 1.1
  Slide titles: Rethink Sans, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.015em, line-height 1.2
  Eyebrows / category pills: Rethink Sans 700, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.04em
  Body: Inter, weight 400, line-height 1.5
Layout: 96px outer margins on every slide. White space is part of the design. Big type. Editorial print magazine feel.
Cards: 2px solid #0a0a0a border, 0px border-radius. No drop shadows. No gradients. No glassmorphism.
Pills: 999px border-radius, only used on eyebrows and CTAs.
Imagery: NO stock photography. NO generic illustrations. NO icons except simple red arrows or chevrons. The ONLY photo allowed is Daniel's portrait — moody, near-black backdrop, desaturated.
Style references: T. Harv Eker's stage materials, Black Belt Launchpad worksheets, Apple keynote 2018-2020, premium print magazines like Monocle.

VOICE RULES (apply to every word of copy):

- One sentence per paragraph.
- Plain English. No corporate jargon.
- NO em dashes anywhere. Use periods.
- NO emojis.
- NO hashtags.
- BANNED words: "10x", "growth hack", "guru", "unlock", "leverage", "hustle", "synergy", "disrupt", "game-changer", "secret sauce", "follow for more".
- Speak as "I" to "you" — never "we" or third person.
- Outcome-first. Use numbers and timeframes.

DECK STRUCTURE (28 slides, generate in this exact order):

1. TITLE — Inverse canvas (#0a0a0a background, #f7f7f8 text). Hero: "How to build a brand that brings high-ticket buyers to you." Subtitle: "Without becoming louder. Without becoming someone you're not." Tiny tag bottom-left: "Daniel Paul · 60 minutes · today". Single red dot top-right.

2. THE QUESTION — Off-white canvas. Big left-aligned: "Why do some people charge $20,000 for the same hour someone else charges $200?" with "$20,000" in red.

3. THE FRAME — Off-white. Two lines stacked: "Today is not about getting more clients." then in red: "Today is about being the person they were already looking for."

4. WHERE I STARTED — Off-white. Left: "Broke. No team. No list. No credibility." in massive black type. Right side: portrait panel with caption "2020. Charging $300 to build websites."

5. THE CROSSOVER — Off-white, centered. "I stopped chasing clients." then in red: "I started building a brand."

6. THE NAMES — Off-white. Eyebrow in red: "SAME STAGE. SAME GREEN ROOM." Then three stacked names in massive black type: "Robert Kiyosaki", "Mac Attram", "Sandy Jadeja". Bottom caption: "18 months later."

7. WHAT I SOLD — Off-white. "I didn't have a better offer." then red: "I had a better brand."

8. THE LESSON — Inverse canvas. Single huge line: "The brand walks in the room before you do."

9. WHAT JUST HAPPENED — Off-white. Stat slide. Eyebrow red: "AI · 2024 → 2026". Hero red stat: "$50,000/yr → $20/mo". Caption: "The price of a skill, today."

10. THE BUNDLING — Off-white. Title top-left: "Skills are being bundled." Three bordered cards: "Copywriting + research + design + editing + coding", "One Claude agent does the work of five juniors.", "Your competitor across the world has the same tools you do. For the same price. Today."

11. THE COLLAPSE — Off-white. Three stacked lines: "Anyone can copy your tone.", "Anyone can copy your offer.", and in red: "Anyone can copy your funnel."

12. THE ONE THING LEFT — Inverse canvas. "Trust is the most expensive asset of 2026." with "expensive" highlighted in red.

13. FRAMEWORK HEADER — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "THE FRAMEWORK". Hero massive: "1 · 1 · 1 · 1". Caption: "One platform. One year. One audience. One call to action."

14. ONE PLATFORM — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "RULE 1 / 4". "Pick one platform." then red: "Stay for one year."

15. WHY ONE PLATFORM WINS — Off-white. Top in silver: "100,000 wrong followers." Divider line. Bottom big near-black: "1,000 right ones." Footer caption: "1,000 right people will pay you more than 100,000 wrong ones."

16. ONE YEAR — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "RULE 2 / 4". "You don't have a content problem." then red: "You have a quitting problem."

17. THE COMPOUND MATH — Off-white. Title: "The honest timeline." Three rows separated by hairlines: "Months 1 – 3 — Invisible." (silver detail), "Months 4 – 9 — The algorithm finds you." (silver detail), "Month 10 onward — Inbound starts." (red highlight on the result).

18. ONE TARGET AUDIENCE — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "RULE 3 / 4". "Stop being useful to everyone." then red: "Be unforgettable to one."

19. THE MIRROR TEST — Off-white. Big red quote mark top-left. Quote in near-black: "If they read your post and don't feel seen, you didn't write it for them."

20. ONE CTA — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "RULE 4 / 4". Three lines: "One ask.", "Repeated quietly.", and in red: "For one year."

21. WHAT A GREAT CTA SOUNDS LIKE — Off-white. Two-card comparison. Left card (black border): eyebrow silver "OFF", body "DM me to learn more." Right card (red border): eyebrow red "ON", body "Comment OPTIMIZER and I'll send you the tool."

22. WHY THE FOUR ONES WORK — Off-white. "Repetition is not boring." then red: "Repetition is recognition." Caption silver: "Same person. Same platform. Same audience. Same ask. For one year."

23. MONDAY MORNING VERSION — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "WHAT THIS LOOKS LIKE". Massive stat: "2 hrs / week." Sub: "From your phone. No team. No agency."

24. THE CADENCE — Off-white. Title: "Three posts a week. Same window." Seven-box calendar row. Mon/Fri/Sat/Sun in light gray with silver text. Tue/Wed/Thu in red fill with off-white "POST" text.

25. THE CONTENT STACK — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "FOREVER STACK". Three equal cards: Card 1 eyebrow "TUE" title "Story" body "One thing that happened to you this week." Card 2 eyebrow "WED" title "Lesson" body "One sharp thing you learned doing your work." Card 3 eyebrow "THU" title "Asset" body "One thing you can give away for free."

26. THE GIFT — Off-white. Eyebrow red: "FROM ME TO YOU". "Your LinkedIn profile is the storefront." then red: "Most of you left the door locked."

27. THE URL — Inverse canvas. Eyebrow uppercase red: "$1,000 LINKEDIN PROFILE AUDIT". Massive URL centered: "linkedinprofile.purelypersonal.ai". Sub silver: "Free. Forever." Bottom-right QR code (generate from https://api.qrserver.com/v1/create-qr-code/?size=320x320&color=0a0a0a&bgcolor=f7f7f8&data=https%3A%2F%2Flinkedinprofile.purelypersonal.ai%2F) on off-white panel, caption: "Scan. Or open it on your phone now."

28. LAST FRAME — Inverse canvas. Big centered: "The brand walks in the room before you do." Sub silver: "Start building yours tonight." Tiny tag: "Daniel Paul · danielpaul.ai"

OUTPUT FORMAT:

- Render every slide exactly once.
- Use only the five brand colors. Reject any palette additions.
- Use only Rethink Sans and Inter. Reject any other font.
- Render all body copy exactly as written above. Do not paraphrase. Do not "improve" the headlines.
- Leave generous whitespace. Big type is the point.
- For slide 4 portrait panel, generate a placeholder labeled "DANNY-PORTRAIT" so the designer can drop the real headshot in.
- For slide 27 QR code, generate a placeholder labeled "QR-LINKEDIN-OPTIMIZER" sized 320x320.

If you cannot render any element exactly as specified, output it as a labeled placeholder rather than substituting your own design.
```

---

## How to use this prompt

1. Open GenSpark Slides AI (genspark.ai) or Gamma or Tome.
2. Paste the entire code block above into the prompt input.
3. Generate.
4. Export to PowerPoint or PDF.
5. Open in Canva. Drop in the real headshot on slide 4. Generate the QR code and drop it on slide 27.

If the tool cannot match the exact layout, the labeled placeholders make it easy to fix in Canva in 10 minutes.
